New Mexico.regulator_name: New Mexico Oil Conservation Division (OCD), EMNRD — with NM Environment Department (NMED) for air emissions
New Mexico.complaint_mechanism: NMED "Report an Environmental Issue or Incident" online form (https://ents.web.env.nm.gov/public/INCIDENT_HDR_add.php); OCD field offices via https://www.emnrd.nm.gov/ocd/contact-us/
New Mexico.applicable_rule: 19.15.27.9 NMAC — Statewide Natural Gas Capture Requirements (part of 19.15.27 NMAC, Venting and Flaring of Natural Gas)
New Mexico.rule_summary: Requires oil and gas operators to capture no less than 98% of produced natural gas by December 31, 2026 (phased from 2022) and prohibits routine venting and flaring except in emergencies or equipment failure. Administered by the Oil Conservation Commission/Division under EMNRD.
New Mexico.salutation: To the New Mexico Oil Conservation Division:

Texas.regulator_name: Railroad Commission of Texas (RRC) — with Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ) for air emissions
Texas.complaint_mechanism: TCEQ environmental complaint form (https://www.tceq.texas.gov/compliance/complaints, 24-hr line 1-888-777-3186); RRC oil and gas complaints via the appropriate district office (https://www.rrc.texas.gov/oil-and-gas/o-g-complaints/)
Texas.applicable_rule: 16 TAC 3.32 — Statewide Rule 32 (Gas Well Gas and Casinghead Gas Disposition; venting and flaring limitations)
Texas.rule_summary: Governs disposition of gas and limits routine venting and flaring but permits flaring by exception/administrative approval. Texas has no state-level mandate comparable to New Mexico 98% gas-capture requirement; MethaneSAT data shows Texas Permian operators emit roughly twice the methane per unit of production versus New Mexico.
Texas.salutation: To the Railroad Commission of Texas:

Oklahoma.regulator_name: Oklahoma Corporation Commission (OCC), Oil and Gas Conservation Division
Oklahoma.complaint_mechanism: OCC Crude Oil & Natural Gas complaint form (https://oklahoma.gov/occ/complaints/crude-oil-natural-gas.html, submission portal https://public.occ.ok.gov/Forms/OGComplaints); Oil and Gas Conservation Division 24/7 line 405-521-2331
Oklahoma.rule_summary: Permits venting or flaring up to 50 mcf/d without a permit when marketing the gas is not economically feasible; larger volumes require an administrative permit (Form 1022), with temporary exemptions during flowback. The rule regulates venting/flaring permits only and sets no affirmative gas-capture requirement, unlike New Mexico 98% capture mandate.
